JScience 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
  1. org.jscience.social.philosophy

Uses of Package
org.jscience.social.philosophy

Packages that use org.jscience.social.philosophy
Package
Description
org.jscience.social.philosophy
Provides support for philosophy, including formal logic, ethics, epistemology, and storytelling.
org.jscience.social.philosophy.epistemology
Provides classes for modeling epistemology, the study of knowledge and justified belief.
org.jscience.social.sociology
- social structures, persons, and cultural modeling.
  • Classes in org.jscience.social.philosophy used by org.jscience.social.philosophy
    Class
    Description
    Belief
    Represents a fundamental conviction or tenet within a philosophical system.
    Concept
    Represents a key idea or element within a philosophical framework.
    DeonticLogicSolver.DeonticMode
    Categories of normative status.
    EthicalFrameworks.Action
    Represents a potential action in an ethical decision-making scenario.
    LogicSolver.Proposition
    Represents a categorical proposition (e.g., "All humans are mortal").
    Model
    Represents a logical organization or cluster of interrelated concepts.
    PhilosophyConstants.Concept
    Fundamental philosophical ontological concepts.
    PhilosophyConstants.ReligionType
    Major types of religious systems.
    Premise
    Represents a logic premise, consisting of a statement and its assigned truth value.
  • Classes in org.jscience.social.philosophy used by org.jscience.social.philosophy.epistemology
    Class
    Description
    Belief
    Represents a fundamental conviction or tenet within a philosophical system.
  • Classes in org.jscience.social.philosophy used by org.jscience.social.sociology
    Class
    Description
    Belief
    Represents a fundamental conviction or tenet within a philosophical system.
